```python
# test_reverse_words.py
import pytest

from your_module import reverse_words  # replace with the actual import path


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "s, expected",
    [
        ("", ""),  # happy-ish: empty string
        ("hello", "hello"),  # single word
        ("hello world", "world hello"),  # two words
        ("the quick brown fox", "fox brown quick the"),  # multiple words
        ("  hello   world  ", "world hello"),  # leading/trailing/multiple spaces
        ("a   b c    d", "d c b a"),  # mixed spacing
        ("\n\t  hello \n world \t", "world hello"),  # any whitespace is treated as separator
    ],
)
def test_reverse_words_happy_paths(s, expected):
    assert reverse_words(s) == expected


def test_reverse_words_collapses_internal_whitespace():
    # Edge case: multiple whitespace between words should be collapsed
    assert reverse_words("a     b") == "b a"


def test_reverse_words_only_whitespace():
    # Edge case: string with only whitespace produces no words => empty output
    assert reverse_words("     ") == ""
    assert reverse_words("\n\t  ") == ""


@pytest.mark.parametrize("s", [None, 123, 1.5, ["a", "b"], {"a": "b"}])
def test_reverse_words_type_errors(s):
    # Error case: function expects str; non-str should raise an exception
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        reverse_words(s)
```
